goal: Add token-bucket rate limiting to the Express API so each client is capped at N requests per window, with over-limit requests rejected via HTTP 429 and a Retry-After header.

items.description: Create the token-bucket rate-limit middleware module that the API can mount.
items.expectedOutput: A new module at src/middleware/rateLimit.js exporting a token-bucket factory (capacity + injected clock source).

items.description: The token-bucket unit tests (refill rate, burst capacity, per-client isolation) pass.
items.expectedOutput: All token-bucket unit tests green under a fake clock that the bucket reads via an injected clock source.

items.description: After exhausting the bucket, the next request over the limit is rejected with HTTP 429. The integration test boots the app, fires N+1 requests from one client, and asserts the (N+1)th status is 429.
items.expectedOutput: The (N+1)th request in a single window returns HTTP 429 once the bucket is empty.

items.description: The 429 response includes a Retry-After header telling the client when to retry. Verified by an integration test that inspects the over-limit response headers.
items.expectedOutput: Every 429 response carries a Retry-After header set to the seconds until the next token refills.

items.description: The complete test suite still passes, confirming rate limiting did not regress existing endpoints.
items.expectedOutput: The full test suite passes with zero regressions in pre-existing endpoints.
